ccel.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

title: To Januarius, Bishop of Caralis (Cagliari).

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: The preacher of Almighty God, Paul the apostle, says, Rebuke not an elder (1 Tim. v.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But this rule of his is to be observed in cases where the fault of an elder does not draw through his example the hearts of the younger into ruin.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, when an elder sets an example to the young for their ruin, he is to be smitten with severe rebuke.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For it is written, Ye are all a snare to the young (Isai. xlii.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And again the prophet says, The sinner being an hundred years old is accursed (Isai. lxv.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But so great wickedness has been reported to us of thy old age that, unless we were humanely disposed, we should smite thee with a definitive curse.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For it has been told me that on the Lord’s day, before celebrating the solemnities of mass, thou wentest forth to plough up the crop of the bearer of these presents, and after ploughing it up didst celebrate the solemnities of mass.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Also, after the solemnities of mass thou didst not fear to root up the landmarks of that possession.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: What punishment ought to follow such deeds all who hear of them know.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: We had, however, been in doubt as to so great perversity in thee as this; but our son Cyriacus the abbot, having been questioned by us, declared that when he was at Caralis he knew it to be the case.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And, seeing that we still spare thy gray hairs, bethink thee at length, old man, and restrain thyself from such levity of behaviour, and perversity of deeds.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: The nearer thou art approaching death, the more careful and fearful oughtest thou to become.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And indeed a sentence of punishment had been launched against thee; but, since we know thy simplicity accompanying thy old age, we meanwhile hold our peace.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Those, however, by whose advice thou hast done these things we decree to be excommunicated for two months; but so that, if within the space of two months anything should happen to them after the manner of humanity they be not deprived of the blessing of the viaticum.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But do thou henceforth be cautious to stand aloof from their counsels, lest, if thou be their disciple in evil whose master thou oughtest to have been in good, we no longer spare either thy simplicity or thy old age.
